reasoning: Based on the search summary, there is no confident evidence that President Trump has already issued a presidential action explicitly mentioning the "Diversity Visa" between his inauguration on Jan 20, 2025 and the knowledge cutoff of May 20, 2025. Although he maintains substantial opposition to the program (often calling it the "Diversity Visa Lottery") and initiated a "whirlwind" of executive actions in his first few months focusing on immigration and DEI, altering or eliminating the Diversity Visa requires congressional action. Moreover, if a major executive order directly targeting the Diversity Visa had been signed, major news outlets would likely have reported on it explicitly. However, the release of the FY 2026 lottery results in May might still prompt an action before the July 1 deadline (e.g., directing agencies to increase vetting or suspending program entry under Section 212(f) powers). Given the limited remaining timeframe (about 40 days) and lack of concrete evidence for an existing action, while balancing his known disdain for the program, the probability is estimated at 25%.
batch_queries: Will President Trump issue a new presidential action mentioning the Diversity Visa before July 1, 2025?
